rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 through KPIs including monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal states that funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions; Provide independent evidence for: The Cardano Builder DAO says it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds. Residual uncertainty: Treasury diligence dossier incomplete; soft gate applied a -0.1 caution penalty and judged on available repo context (anchor + doctrine + reasoning lean) rather than abstaining. Confidence context: score -0.51; confidence 100.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.030 (clamped to ±0.05): The extracted claims show a plausible public-benefit funding mechanism and some prior operating history, but most high-materiality track-record and KPI-effectiveness claims are proposer asserted in the anchor, while the deterministic assessment reports missing budget, feasibility, risk, alternatives, and failure-mode analysis for a 20,000,000 ADA treasury withdrawal. Under the doctrine's caution around treasury stewardship and evidence quality, a small negative nudge is justified beyond the mechanical blockers.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal states that funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.
Provide independent evidence for: The Cardano Builder DAO says it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal for the Cardano Builder DAO, described as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism to allocate capital to builders advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs.
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO / independent dRep DAO council administrator prior to milestone-based disbursement

Deliverables: fund builders expected to improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL, operate a builder-led DAO proposal review, feedback, voting, and funding process, require funded projects to report core KPIs, continue development of KPI tracking toward live on-chain Cardano data, disburse funds only after milestone completion is validated by an independent administrator

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 through KPIs including monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The DAO has built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 1 using public self-reported KPI tracking and Version 2 moving toward live on-chain Cardano data.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO says it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Round 1 is described as involving 38 voting members, 27 requesting members, 20 funded companies, an 83% governance participation rate, and 5,541,335 ADA distributed through a transparent smart contract-governed process.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Round 2 is described as adding 18 new members, funding 14 companies, and achieving an 88% member participation rate after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states that funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.
Independent evidence for: The DAO has built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 1 using public self-reported KPI tracking and Version 2 moving toward live on-chain Cardano data.
Independent evidence for: The Cardano Builder DAO says it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Independent evidence for: Round 1 is described as involving 38 voting members, 27 requesting members, 20 funded companies, an 83% governance participation rate, and 5,541,335 ADA distributed through a transparent smart contract-governed process.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 through KPIs including monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
